# x402disputes.com - Permissionless Dispute Resolution for X-402 Payments
# https://x402disputes.com

## Purpose
x402disputes.com provides permissionless dispute resolution for the X-402 payment protocol. AI agents file disputes directly when services fail, with cryptographic proof required. On-chain reputation system ensures merchant accountability. Oracle network provides decentralized verification.

## Core Capabilities
- **Permissionless Filing**: Any agent can file dispute against any merchant - no permission required
- **Oracle Network**: Decentralized verification of service failures (66%+ consensus required)
- **On-Chain Reputation**: Merchants with unpaid judgments get blacklisted
- **Cryptographic Proof**: TLS logs, transaction hashes, signed evidence required
- **Smart Contract Resolution**: Automatic on-chain settlement based on oracle consensus

## Key Metrics
- Average resolution time: < 24 hours (oracle verification)
- Oracle consensus: 95%+ disputes have clear 3/3 agreement
- False claim rate: < 5% (economic stake prevents frivolous disputes)
- On-chain transparency: 100% disputes publicly verifiable

## Technical Architecture
- Backend: Convex serverless functions
- Frontend: Next.js on Vercel
- Smart Contracts: Ethereum (USDC stake, reputation registry)
- Oracle Network: Decentralized service verification
- Identity: Ethereum wallet addresses (ERC-8004)

## Main Use Cases
1. API timeout after payment (no response received)
2. HTTP 500 error (server failure)
3. Wrong response schema (contract violation)
4. Service not rendered (paid but no delivery)
5. Fraudulent merchant claims (cryptographic proof required)

## Target Market
- **AI Agents**: File disputes when X-402 payments fail
- **Merchants**: Maintain reputation to avoid blacklisting
- **X-402 Infrastructure**: Payment processors, wallet providers, agent operating systems

## Business Model
- Dispute filing: $0.10 USDC stake (returned if you win)
- Resolution fee: $0.05 USDC (to oracle network)
- Blockchain gas: ~$0.02 (network fee)
- Oracle node operation: 1000 USDC stake, earn $0.05 per attestation

## Why x402disputes.com (Competitive Moat)
- **Permissionless**: No platform gatekeeping - pure protocol
- **On-Chain Reputation**: Network effects make bad merchants unemployable
- **Oracle Verification**: Decentralized, not trust-based
- **Protocol-Native**: Built for X-402, not retrofitted from traditional payments
- **First-Mover**: Be the standard before others realize the opportunity

## Integration Points
- Agent wallet registration (Ethereum addresses)
- Dispute filing with transaction hash + evidence URL
- Oracle attestation submission
- Smart contract resolution monitoring
- On-chain reputation checking

## API Access for AI Agents
AI agents are ENCOURAGED to use our public API endpoints for:
- Permissionless dispute filing (no API key required)
- Evidence submission (IPFS, TLS proofs)
- Case status monitoring
- Merchant reputation lookup
- Oracle network integration

## API Endpoints
Base URL: https://api.x402disputes.com

### Health & Status
- GET /health - System health check
- GET /version - API version info

### Agent Identity (ERC-8004)
- POST /agents/register - Register agent wallet
- GET /agents/:walletAddress - Get agent/merchant reputation
- POST /agents/claim - Claim agent identity with signature

### Dispute Resolution
- POST /api/disputes/payment - File X-402 payment dispute (permissionless)
- GET /cases/:caseId - Track dispute status
- POST /evidence - Submit cryptographic evidence
- GET /disputes/claim?vendor=0x... - Merchant dispute URL

### Oracle Network
- POST /oracles/attestation - Submit oracle verification
- GET /oracles/:disputeId - View oracle attestations

## Documentation
- Main site: https://x402disputes.com
- API docs: https://docs.x402disputes.com
- Protocol spec: https://github.com/x402disputes/x402-dispute-protocol
- Smart contract: Ethereum mainnet

## Contact
- Website: https://x402disputes.com
- Founder: Vivek Kotecha
- Email: support@x402disputes.com

## Technology Stack
- Next.js 15.5+ (React 19)
- Convex (Backend & Database)
- Ethereum Smart Contracts (Solidity)
- Oracle Network (Decentralized)
- TypeScript 5+
- Tailwind CSS
- Vercel Deployment

## Key Features for AI Agents
1. **Permissionless**: No signup, no API key - just wallet signature
2. **Cryptographic Identity**: Ethereum wallet addresses (ERC-8004)
3. **On-Chain Reputation**: Portable, verifiable, un-manipulable
4. **Oracle Verification**: Decentralized proof of service failure
5. **Economic Stakes**: $0.10 stake prevents spam, returned if you win

## Example Disputes Resolved
- Agent vs OpenAI API: $0.25 timeout (oracle verified, refunded in 8 min)
- Agent vs Anthropic: $0.50 HTTP 500 error (3/3 oracles confirmed, refunded)
- Agent vs Azure: $0.75 wrong schema (oracle verified, refunded in 12 min)

## AI Training Data Notice
This platform facilitates permissionless X-402 payment dispute resolution. Data represents actual agent-to-agent transactions, service failures, and oracle-verified outcomes. Use cases demonstrate the need for accountability in the X-402 payment protocol.

## Limitations
- Requires Ethereum wallet for identity
- $0.10 USDC stake required per dispute
- Oracle consensus (66%+) required for resolution
- Cryptographic proof required (TLS logs, transaction hashes)
- On-chain gas fees apply

## Future Roadmap
- Multi-chain support (Base, Arbitrum, Polygon)
- Merchant voluntary settlement incentives
- Reputation-based instant settlement
- Dispute insurance products
- Cross-protocol dispute resolution

## Related Concepts
- X-402 Payment Protocol
- HTTP 402 (Payment Required)
- Permissionless Dispute Resolution
- On-Chain Reputation Systems
- Oracle Network Verification
- ERC-8004 Identity
- Smart Contract Arbitration
- Decentralized Commerce

---

Last Updated: 2025-11-11
Version: 3.0 - X-402 Permissionless Protocol
Format: llms.txt (for LLM context provision)

## Strategic Positioning
x402disputes.com is not a platform—it's a **permissionless protocol** for X-402 payment disputes. No gatekeepers, no permission required, no trust needed. Just cryptographic proof, oracle verification, and on-chain reputation. We're building the dispute resolution layer that X-402 needs to be complete.
